title: agropyron cristatum subsp. brachyatherum
intro: Agropyron cristatum subsp. brachyatherum, also known as Tibetan wheatgrass or Tibetan wildrye, is a subspecies of grass native to the high-altitude regions of the Himalayas and Tibet. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Agropyron cristatum subsp. brachyatherum include reducing stress and anxiety, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, capsules, or tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
